ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names Vegeta and the Super Saiyan 2 form. The card title is the primary identifier — this is a DBS TCG card where the depicted character is named in the card title itself, which meets the explicit-text standard for IP verification. Visual consistency (SSJ2 golden aura, double-blast stance, dark-blue armor palette) is compatible with Vegeta's known appearance.

trivia_bullets.text: Union Force (set code BT2, product DBS-B02) released November 3, 2017 as the second booster set for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game. The set covers multiple sagas including the Android/Cell arc, Future Trunks arc, Majin Buu Saga, and Tournament of Power. BT2-010 falls in the Majin Buu Saga grouping, placing Vegeta in his SS2 form as seen in the Babidi arc. [WebSearch: Bleeding Cool DBS Union Force Checklist; dbs-cardgame.com product page]
sources: WebSearch: Bleeding Cool DBS Union Force checklist

trivia_bullets.text: BT2-010 is a Common rarity Red Battle card with 15,000 power, energy cost 3, and the skill: at end of battle after comboing from hand, if your Leader is red, play this card in Rest Mode — an aggressive combo extender. The card's Saiyan special trait and Majin Buu Saga tag place it in Red Saiyan aggro archetypes from the game's early competitive meta. [WebSearch: TrollAndToad BT2-010 product listing; CoreTCG listing]

In Dragon Ball Z canon, Vegeta's true Super Saiyan 2 form debuted during the Majin Buu Saga — distinct from his Cell arc Super Saiyan Grade 2 ("Super Vegeta"), which was not the genuine SS2. The card's Majin Buu Saga tag is lore-accurate: Vegeta achieved SS2 through years of post-Cell training, then accepted Babidi's Majin mark to push further, leading to his final battle with Goku before Buu's revival. [WebSearch: ComicBook.com — Dragon Ball doubles down on Vegeta's SS2 origin; WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki Majin Buu Saga]
sources: WebSearch: ComicBook.com — Vegeta Super Saiyan 2 origin
WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki Fandom — Majin Buu Saga

trivia_bullets.text: BT2-010 also exists as a Judge Promotion Card variant (Level 1 and Level 2 judge promos), making this common print one of several parallel versions of the same card. The judge promo versions are notably more collectible. [WebSearch: MnaCardz BT2-010 Judge Lvl 1; WebSearch: GatheringPointGames BT2-010 Judge Lvl 2]
sources: WebSearch: MnaCardz BT2-010 C Judge LVL 1 listing
WebSearch: GatheringPointGames BT2-010 Judge Promotion listing
